proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear effect).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable OLS: beta=+0.176 months per year, p<1e-300 (Spearman r=+0.785).
Univariable OLS: beta=-1.190 per ECOG point, p<1e-300.
Mean PFS 5.05 (stage_iv=0) vs 3.69 (stage_iv=1); diff=-1.35 months, p<1e-300.
beta=+0.465 per g/dL, p=3e-115.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: beta=-0.0053 per ng/mL, p=3e-17 (Spearman r=-0.047).
Mean PFS 4.31 (female) vs 4.31 (male); diff=-0.006, p=0.77 (not significant).
beta=-0.0020 per mg/L, p=0.065 (borderline).
beta=+0.005, p=0.29 (not significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
Patients with nras_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have longer pfs_months than msi_high=0.
Patients with her2_amplified=1 have shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
Patients with ntrk_fusion=1 have a different pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.
Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.17 (kras=1) vs 4.50 (kras=0); diff=-0.327, p=2e-58.
Mean PFS 4.09 (braf=1) vs 4.32 (braf=0); diff=-0.228, p=3e-7.
Mean PFS 4.53 (nras=1) vs 4.30 (nras=0); diff=+0.221, p=7e-4. Sign opposite to predicted: NRAS-mutant patients had LONGER PFS.
Mean PFS 4.29 (msi=1) vs 4.31 (msi=0); diff=-0.019, p=0.68 (not significant).
Mean PFS 4.26 (her2=1) vs 4.31 (her2=0); diff=-0.056, p=0.36 (not significant).
Mean PFS 4.35 (ntrk=1) vs 4.31 (ntrk=0); diff=+0.040, p=0.79 (not significant).
Mean PFS 4.10 (right=1) vs 4.41 (right=0); diff=-0.305, p=1e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_cetuximab and patients off treatment_cetuximab (overall, no specific direction).
Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_bevacizumab and patients off treatment_bevacizumab (overall, no specific direction).
Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_pembrolizumab and patients off treatment_pembrolizumab (overall, no specific direction).
Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_encorafenib and patients off treatment_encorafenib (overall, no specific direction).
Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib and patients off treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib (overall, no specific direction).
Patients on treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_regorafenib (overall positive effect).

analyses.result_summary: TE=-0.019 months (4.30 on vs 4.32 off), p=0.35.
TE=+0.972 months (5.09 on vs 4.12 off), p<1e-200.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment for every treatment column is independent of every biomarker (kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion); biomarker frequencies do not differ between treated and untreated for any treatment.

analyses.code: Cross-tabs of each treatment vs each biomarker; biomarker prevalence in treated vs untreated
analyses.result_summary: Across all 6 treatments x 6 biomarkers, biomarker prevalence is within +/-0.01 of the overall rate (cetuximab: 0.415 vs 0.419 KRAS; pembrolizumab: 0.052 vs 0.050 MSI-high; encorafenib: 0.047 vs 0.045 BRAF; trastuzumab/tucatinib: 0.030 vs 0.030 HER2). No biomarker enrichment in any treatment arm; assignments are effectively random with respect to biomarkers, so any treatment x biomarker interaction reflects real heterogeneity rather than confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, and laboratory features in a single OLS model, the dominant prognostic signals on pfs_months are age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), cea_ng_ml (negative), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), and ldh_u_l (negative).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + sex + biomarkers + labs
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.83. age beta=+0.176 p<1e-300; ecog beta=-1.18 p<1e-300; stage_iv beta=-1.36 p<1e-300; albumin beta=+0.46 p<1e-300; cea beta=-0.005 p=1e-81; weight_loss beta=-0.076 p<1e-300; ldh beta=-0.0004 p=7e-16; right_sided beta=-0.29 p=8e-235; kras beta=-0.35 p<1e-300; braf beta=-0.35 p=2e-64. Sex, MSI, HER2, NTRK, NLR, CRP, hemoglobin, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium NOT independently significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 patients (the canonical RAS/RAF wild-type indication for anti-EGFR therapy).
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in msi_high=1 patients.
treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in braf_v600e=1 patients.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in her2_amplified=1 patients.

analyses.code: Subset to KRAS=NRAS=BRAF=0; t-test pfs by treatment_cetuximab
analyses.result_summary: Within RAS/RAF wild-type (n=25,324): TE=-0.045 months, p=0.17. NO significant cetuximab benefit even in the canonical indication. Refutes the hypothesis.

analyses.code: Subset to msi_high=1; t-test pfs by treatment_pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Within MSI-high (n=2,513): TE=+0.007 months, p=0.96. NO pembrolizumab benefit even in MSI-high. Refutes the hypothesis.

analyses.code: Subset to braf_v600e=1; t-test pfs by treatment_encorafenib
analyses.result_summary: Within BRAF V600E (n=2,272): TE=-0.129 months, p=0.33. NO encorafenib benefit. Refutes the hypothesis.

analyses.code: Subset to her2_amplified=1; t-test pfs by treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib
analyses.result_summary: Within HER2-amplified (n=1,504): TE=+0.017 months, p=0.93. NO trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit. Refutes the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in kras_mutation=0 than in kras_mutation=1 (regorafenib benefit concentrated in KRAS wild-type).
The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=0 than in braf_v600e=1.
The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided primaries) than in right_sided_primary=1.
The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0.
The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in nras_mutation=1 than in nras_mutation=0.

analyses.result_summary: TE in kras=0: +1.66 months; TE in kras=1: +0.03; interaction beta=-1.629, p=8e-226. Strongly supports.
TE in braf=0: +1.03; TE in braf=1: -0.16; interaction beta=-1.183, p=6e-23.
TE in right=0: +1.48; TE in right=1: +0.03; interaction beta=-1.454, p=4e-168.
TE in msi=0: +0.97; TE in msi=1: +1.04; interaction beta=+0.07, p=0.71. No heterogeneity by MSI.
TE in nras=0: +0.94; TE in nras=1: +1.93; interaction beta=+0.99, p=1e-11. NRAS-mutant patients (always KRAS-WT in this cohort) gain extra benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint stratification of kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary, the treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit is restricted to the cell with kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided primary); in every other cell the benefit is essentially zero.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests in 6 KRAS x BRAF x sidedness cells
analyses.result_summary: KRAS=0,BRAF=0,left: TE=+2.76 (n_t=3461) p<1e-300. KRAS=0,BRAF=1,left: TE=-0.20 p=0.12. KRAS=0,BRAF=0,right: TE=+0.08 p=0.12. KRAS=0,BRAF=1,right: TE=-0.07 p=0.70. KRAS=1,BRAF=0,left: TE=+0.06 p=0.17. KRAS=1,BRAF=0,right: TE=-0.04 p=0.52. The benefit is concentrated entirely in the KRAS=0/BRAF=0/left cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The kras_mutation main effect on pfs_months that we detected in iteration 3 is entirely explained by differential regorafenib benefit; among patients with treatment_regorafenib=0, kras_mutation has no independent prognostic effect on pfs_months.
The braf_v600e main effect on pfs_months disappears among patients with treatment_regorafenib=0.
The right_sided_primary main effect on pfs_months disappears among patients with treatment_regorafenib=0.

analyses.code: t-test pfs by kras_mutation in df[treatment_regorafenib==0]
analyses.result_summary: n=39,978: TE=-0.001 months, p=0.95. KRAS not prognostic absent regorafenib.

analyses.code: t-test pfs by braf_v600e in non-rego subset
analyses.result_summary: TE=+0.010, p=0.85. BRAF not prognostic absent regorafenib.

analyses.code: t-test pfs by right_sided_primary in non-rego subset
analyses.result_summary: TE=-0.012, p=0.59. Right-sidedness not prognostic absent regorafenib.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After fitting an adjusted OLS model with prognostic covariates plus indicators for regorafenib_within_eligible (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) and regorafenib_outside_eligible, the within-eligible regorafenib coefficient is large and positive (~+2.7 months) while the outside-eligible regorafenib coefficient is essentially zero. The kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary main-effect coefficients become non-significant.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + right + kras + braf + cea + albumin + weight_loss + ldh + rego_in_eligible + rego_outside_eligible
analyses.result_summary: rego_in_eligible beta=+2.74 (p<1e-300); rego_outside_eligible beta=-0.002 (p=0.79). Adjusted KRAS beta=-0.001 p=0.91; BRAF beta=+0.003 p=0.85; right beta=+0.002 p=0.71. The apparent KRAS/BRAF/right prognostic effects are fully explained by the regorafenib heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all patients, lower cea_ng_ml predicts a larger treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months (negative regorafenib x cea interaction).
Across all patients, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months varies with age_years.
Across all patients, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months varies with albumin_g_dl.
Across all patients, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months varies with ldh_u_l.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ rego + cea + rego:cea (all patients)
analyses.result_summary: Median-split: TE_low_CEA=+1.81, TE_high_CEA=+0.14. Interaction beta=-0.025 per ng/mL, p=9e-58. Strongly supports.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ rego + age + rego:age (all patients)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta non-significant (p > 0.05).
Interaction not significant (p=0.13).
Interaction not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 patients, lower cea_ng_ml predicts a larger treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit; the interaction between regorafenib and CEA is negative inside this eligibility subgroup.
Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not vary by age_years.
Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not vary by ecog_ps.
Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not vary by stage_iv.
Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not differ between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ rego + cea + rego:cea in df[eligible]
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible (n=17,372): rego beta=+3.35, cea beta=+0.0002, rego:cea beta=-0.065 (p=7e-136). Massive negative interaction with CEA inside the eligible subgroup.
TE_high_age=+2.79, TE_low_age=+2.73; p_int=0.29. No age modification.

analyses.code: Stratify TE by ecog 0/1/2 in eligible
analyses.result_summary: TE: ECOG=0 +2.66, ECOG=1 +2.77, ECOG=2 +2.74. No ECOG modification.

analyses.code: Stratify TE by stage_iv in eligible
analyses.result_summary: TE: stage=0 +2.62, stage=1 +2.86. Modest, not clearly meaningful.

analyses.code: Stratify TE by sex_female in eligible
analyses.result_summary: TE: male +2.62, female +2.93. Modest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligibility subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) there is a sharp threshold on cea_ng_ml at approximately 5 ng/mL: patients with cea_ng_ml < 5 derive a treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit of approximately +5 months, while patients with cea_ng_ml >= 5 derive essentially zero benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests across CEA thresholds within eligible
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible: CEA<3 TE=+5.03 (n=6436); CEA<4 TE=+5.04; CEA<5 TE=+5.01 (n=9326); CEA>=5 TE=+0.005 (n=8046, p=0.99). Threshold at CEA=5 ng/mL is sharp and clinically interpretable (upper-normal range).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete regorafenib responder subgroup is kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; within this subgroup treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months, while outside the subgroup the effect is approximately zero.

analyses.code: t-test pfs by treatment_regorafenib within and outside the 4-feature subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Inside (n=9,326; n_treated=1,903): TE=+5.013 months, p<1e-300. Outside (n=40,674; n_treated=8,119): TE=+0.025 months, p=0.33. Subgroup definition is complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across two-feature subgroups defined by binary clinical/biomarker variables, no subgroup yields a clinically meaningful (>=0.3 month) or statistically significant treatment_cetuximab pfs_months benefit.
treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, left-sided).

analyses.code: Two-feature subgroup grid (biomarkers, sidedness, stage, sex)
analyses.result_summary: Top TEs (with reasonable n) all <0.45 months and not significant after multiple comparisons. Best raw TE +0.42 (ntrk=1 & female, n=33+91, p>0.1).

analyses.code: t-test pfs by treatment_cetuximab in kras=0 & right=0
analyses.result_summary: n_t=5067, n_u=11376; TE=-0.067 months, p=0.13. Refutes the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across two-feature subgroups of binary clinical/biomarker variables, treatment_bevacizumab does not produce a meaningful pfs_months benefit; the only nominally significant signal is a small NEGATIVE effect of treatment_bevacizumab in braf_v600e=1 patients.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests across MSI, KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, stage, sex
analyses.result_summary: All TE estimates between -0.24 and +0.11 months. The single nominally significant cell is BRAF V600E (TE=-0.237, p=0.006), which would not survive multiple-testing correction across the full grid.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, no continuous lab feature (cea, albumin, ldh, weight_loss, crp, nlr, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) defines a subgroup with a meaningful treatment_pembrolizumab pfs_months benefit.
Within msi_high=1 patients, the treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months does not differ between cea_ng_ml above vs below the median.

analyses.code: Median-split each continuous lab in msi_high=1; t-test pfs by pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: All TEs in MSI-high subgroups remain near zero (|TE|<0.15 months) for every continuous-modifier split. No subgroup with a meaningful pembrolizumab benefit identified.

analyses.code: Median-split CEA in msi_high=1; pembrolizumab TE comparison
analyses.result_summary: TE_low_CEA and TE_high_CEA both <0.05 months, neither significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within braf_v600e=1 patients, no second feature (msi_high, kras_mutation, right_sided_primary, sex_female, stage_iv, ecog_ps, age_years, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo) defines a subgroup with a meaningful treatment_encorafenib pfs_months benefit.

analyses.result_summary: All BRAF V600E sub-subgroup TEs lie between -0.5 and +0.5 months and none are statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within her2_amplified=1 patients, no additional binary or continuous feature defines a subgroup with a meaningful treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib pfs_months benefit.

analyses.result_summary: All HER2+ sub-subgroup TEs are tiny (|TE|<0.25). No subgroup achieves a meaningful pfs_months benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients receiving treatment_regorafenib, additionally receiving treatment_bevacizumab does not modify the pfs_months effect of regorafenib (no synergistic interaction).
Among patients receiving treatment_regorafenib, additionally receiving treatment_cetuximab does not modify the pfs_months effect of regorafenib (no synergistic interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient ~0; not significant. Bevacizumab does not modify regorafenib effect.
Interaction coefficient ~0; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When pfs_months is regressed on (treatment_regorafenib, eligibility_subgroup, regorafenib*eligibility), the regorafenib*eligibility interaction coefficient is approximately +2.7 months and dwarfs the regorafenib main effect, confirming that the entire regorafenib signal is concentrated in the eligibility subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: rego beta=+0.030 p=0.32; eligibility beta=+0.001 p=0.98; rego:eligibility beta=+2.728 p<1e-300. The interaction term carries the entire regorafenib signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, cea_ng_ml, and ldh_u_l, age_years remains POSITIVELY associated with pfs_months (beta > 0, statistically significant).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + albumin + weight_loss + cea + ldh
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age beta=+0.176 per year, p<1e-300. The univariable positive direction is preserved after controlling for major prognostic covariates. Direction is unusual clinically but consistent within this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined consolidated statement: the only treatment in this cohort that confers a positive pfs_months effect is treatment_regorafenib, and that effect is fully concentrated in the four-feature subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; in this subgroup the regorafenib pfs_months effect is approximately +5.0 months, while outside the subgroup the effect is approximately zero. The other five treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) show no clinically or statistically meaningful pfs_months benefit anywhere, including in their canonical biomarker-defined indications.

analyses.code: Combined verification: subgroup TE, multivariable adjustment, and exhaustive search across alternatives
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup TE +5.01 (p<1e-300), outside +0.025 (p=0.33). Multivariable interaction beta +2.73 (p<1e-300). Exhaustive search of two-feature subgroups for the other five treatments yielded no TE with |TE|>0.5 months at meaningful sample sizes. The refined hypothesis is fully supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for pfs_months. Treatment: treatment_regorafenib. Direction: increases pfs_months. Subgroup predicates (must all hold): kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5. Within this subgroup the treatment effect on pfs_months is approximately +5.0 months. If any one of the four predicates fails (i.e. kras_mutation=1, OR braf_v600e=1, OR right_sided_primary=1, OR cea_ng_ml>=5) the regorafenib treatment effect on pfs_months is suppressed to approximately 0. Each of the four predicates is necessary; none alone is sufficient.

FINAL FINDING for the other treatments: there is no patient subgroup in this cohort within which treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces a meaningful (>=0.3 month) and statistically defensible pfs_months benefit; their canonical biomarker-targeted indications (RAS/RAF wild-type, MSI-high, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified) all show null effects in this dataset.

analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (kras=0 & braf=0 & right=0 & cea<5; n=9,326): regorafenib TE=+5.013 months (p<1e-300). Outside (n=40,674): TE=+0.025 months (p=0.33).

Cetuximab in RAS/RAF WT: TE=-0.045 p=0.17. Pembrolizumab in MSI-high: TE=+0.007 p=0.96. Encorafenib in BRAF V600E: TE=-0.129 p=0.33. Trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2+: TE=+0.017 p=0.93. Bevacizumab overall: TE=-0.019 p=0.35. None shows a meaningful PFS benefit in any examined subgroup.
